mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 3-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 0.5%, signal central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, which drives corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by declining unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% and rising payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k, signals stronger consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, which lowers corporate input costs and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and business investment (INDPRO) indicates weakening demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-contracting periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite tightening signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market slowdown, indicated by declining PAYEMS and rising UNRATE, signals reduced corporate hiring and consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in co-declining periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
